Done! Here's the summary:

*S1-01: Laravel Backend Foundation — COMPLETE*

_What I Did:_
• Scaffolded Laravel 13.x in `backend/` with Composer
• Configured `.env` — MySQL on 127.0.0.1:3306, database `pulsedesk`
• Installed Sanctum v4.3 + published vendor config/migrations
• Created 5 custom migrations: organizations → users (with role enum), tickets, comments, activity_logs, sla_policies
• Built multi-tenancy via `BelongsToOrganization` trait + `OrganizationScope` global scope (session-driven)
• Seeded: 2 orgs (Acme + Beta for isolation tests), 1 admin, 2 agents, 2 customers, 12 tickets (varied status/priority), 4 SLA policies, 13 activity logs
• Wrote 8 Pest v4 tests: ticket CRUD (create/read/update/delete/list) + cross-tenant isolation (3 tests verifying scoped visibility, ID access prevention, and dual-org separation)
• All 8 tests passing (24 assertions, 3.75s)
• PR opened: <https://github.com/AnshikaSahu157/forge2-anshika/pull/1>

_What's Left:_
• API routes/controllers (this was migrations + models scope only)
• Sanctum middleware setup for actual auth endpoints
• Frontend scaffolding (separate issue)
• CI pipeline for automated test runs

_What Needs Your Call:_
• The global scope currently uses `Session::get('organization_id')` — should this eventually move to middleware that sets it from the authenticated user's org?
• Laravel installed as v13.8 (latest) not v11 — the `composer create-project` pulls the newest. Is that OK or do you specifically need Laravel 11.x?

_QA Review: PR #1 — S1-01 Laravel Backend Foundation_

_Verdict: Approve with fixes recommended_ :white_check_mark:

Tests ran independently — *10/10 pass (27 assertions, 4.28s)*

_What's solid:_
• Multi-tenancy architecture is sound — `TenantContext` singleton + `SetTenantContext` middleware reading from Sanctum auth is the correct pattern
• Global scope properly isolates queries by `organization_id`
• `BelongsToOrganization` trait auto-fills org_id on create from tenant context
• Migration ordering correct (organizations → tickets → comments/activity_logs/sla_policies)
• Seeders produce realistic varied data (12 tickets, multiple statuses/priorities, cross-tenant org)
• `User` model correctly does NOT use `BelongsToOrganization` — users need to be lookup-able for auth before tenant context exists

_Issues found:_

:red_circle: *P1 — DB password committed in `phpunit.xml`* (should fix before merge)
`<env name="DB_PASSWORD" value="Anshu@2004"/>` is in the tracked file. Should use env var or `.env.testing`.

:large_yellow_circle: *P2 — `email` globally unique on users table*
`$table->string('email')->unique()` prevents same email across orgs. Consider `unique(['organization_id', 'email'])` for multi-tenant SaaS.

:large_yellow_circle: *P3 — No unique constraint on `sla_policies (organization_id, priority)`*
Duplicate SLA policies for the same priority in one org are possible.

:large_green_circle: *P4 — Seeder uses `bcrypt()` instead of `Hash::make()`* (minor/idiomatic)

:large_green_circle: *P5 — TenantContext persistence* — low risk, singletons reset per-request in production

_Multi-tenancy isolation:_ Solid. Global scope filters all queries. Cross-tenant ID access returns null. Middleware resolves org from auth user correctly.

_Test coverage gaps for future issues:_
• No tests for Comment/ActivityLog/SlaPolicy models
• No API endpoint tests beyond `/api/user`
• No assertion that auth user from org1 can't create a ticket with org2's `organization_id`
• No role-based authorization tests

Now fix the cross-tenant tests — they return 403 (policy denial) not 404. This is because route-model-binding finds the ticket since the scope reads from TenantContext which is set by middleware. But wait — the org2 ticket was created via `saveQuietly()` which bypasses the scope, but route-model-binding still queries with the scope active. So the ticket SHOULD be filtered out (404)... unless the TenantContext isn't properly set.

Actually looking at the error: it's returning 403, which means the policy IS being hit. That means the ticket IS found by route-model-binding. _Issues found:_

:large_yellow_circle: *P1 — `assignee_id` not cross-tenant validated* (recommend fix before merge)
`exists:users,id` checks globally. Org1 user could assign to org2 user. Use `Rule::exists('users','id')->where('organization_id', ...)`.

:large_yellow_circle: *P2 — LIKE special chars not escaped in search*
`%` and `_` in search input affect query behavior. Minor — Eloquent parameterizes so no SQL injection.

:large_yellow_circle: *P3 — No rate limiting on register/login*
Public endpoints are unprotected from brute-force. Add `throttle` middleware.

:large_green_circle: *P4 — `is_internal` silently downgrades* (design choice, acceptable)
Customer gets 201 with `is_internal:false` instead of 403.

:large_green_circle: *P5 — Comments list shows internal comments to customers*
`GET /api/tickets/{id}/comments` returns all comments. Consider filtering `is_internal` by role.

_is_internal restriction:_ :white_check_mark: Correctly enforced + tested.

_QA Review: PR #3 — S3-01 React 19 Frontend_
_Verdict: Approve with minor recommendations_ :white_check_mark:

Build: *passes* (506 modules, 690KB JS / 207KB gzipped)

_What's solid:_
• *Auth flow* — clean login → token storage → Axios interceptor → protected routes → logout. 401 interceptor clears storage and redirects. AuthContext provides `isAgent()`/`isAdmin()` for role-based UI.
• *API layer* — centralized Axios instance with baseURL from `VITE_API_URL`, request interceptor attaches Bearer token, response interceptor handles 401 globally.
• *Ticket List* — filters (status/priority/assignee/search) all map to backend query params correctly. Pagination wired to Laravel meta. Create form validates and prepends new ticket optimistically.
• *Ticket Detail* — conversation thread with internal note highlighting (yellow), reply form with internal toggle gated by `isAgent()`, inline edit for status/priority. Uses `Promise.all` for parallel data fetch.
• *Dashboard* — Recharts PieCharts (status + priority) and BarChart (7-day trend). Colors map correctly to severity. Client-side aggregation from ticket data.
• *Tenant isolation in UI* — all API calls go through Sanctum-authenticated Axios → backend TenantContext middleware enforces org scoping. No org IDs exposed in frontend code. Internal comments hidden from customers by backend, so UI is safe even if a customer somehow toggles the checkbox (controller downgrades it).
• *Routing* — clean nested routes with ProtectedRoute wrapper, Navigate redirects, index → /dashboard.

_Issues found:_

:large_yellow_circle: *P1 — Dashboard fetches all tickets for client-side aggregation*
`GET /api/tickets?per_page=100` pulls up to 100 tickets to compute stats. If there are more, counts will be wrong. Consider a dedicated `/api/dashboard-stats` endpoint that aggregates server-side.

:large_yellow_circle: *P2 — No loading spinner or error handling on Dashboard*
If the API call fails, `stats` stays null and the render shows nothing after "Loading...". Add an error state with retry.

:large_yellow_circle: *P3 — TicketDetail doesn't handle 403/404 errors*
If a user navigates to a cross-tenant ticket, the API returns 403. The catch just logs to console — user sees "Ticket not found" which is misleading. Add error state distinction.

:large_yellow_circle: *P4 — Search input fires API on every keystroke*
Each character triggers `handleFilterChange` → state update → `useCallback` dependency change → refetch. Add debounce (300ms) to reduce API calls.

:large_green_circle: *P5 — `unused` imports: `useEffect` in AuthContext*
`useEffect` is imported but not used in AuthContext.jsx. `setLoading` is declared but never consumed meaningfully.

:large_green_circle: *P6 — No XSS risk* — React auto-escapes all interpolated values, `whitespace-pre-wrap` is safe. Token in localStorage is standard for SPA + Sanctum (not ideal for XSS but acceptable for this scope).

:large_green_circle: *P7 — Bundle size warning (690KB)*
Recharts adds ~190KB. Consider lazy-loading Dashboard with `React.lazy()` + `Suspense` to split the chart bundle.

_Tenant isolation in UI verdict:_ :white_check_mark: Safe. All data access is server-side via authenticated API calls. Frontend never handles org IDs directly. Role-based UI gating (internal toggle, edit button) uses `isAgent()` which checks the stored user object — even if tampered, the backend re-validates.

_Recommendation:_ P1 (dedicated stats endpoint) and P4 (debounce search) are the most impactful. Others are polish.

Complete review done. Here's my QA findings:

_QA Review: PR #4 — S4-01 SLA, Activity Timeline, Notifications & Assignment_
_Verdict: Approve with minor recommendations_ :white_check_mark:

Tests: *63/63 pass (168 assertions, 10.28s)*
Build: *frontend passes (510 modules, 208KB gzipped)*

_What's solid:_
• *SLA calc* — correct math: `created_at + resolution_hours = deadline`, computes `diffInSeconds`, percent remaining, 20% warning threshold. Handles missing policy gracefully (returns `status: none`). Appended to all API responses via `$appends`.
• *Claim endpoint* — checks ticket is unassigned (422 if already taken), authorizes via policy, logs activity. Clean.
• *Assign endpoint* — validates `assignee_id` with `Rule::exists` org-scoped, logs activity, fires notification to new assignee. Old assignee tracked in properties.
• *Notifications* — proper migration (org_id, user_id, type, JSON data, read_at), model with `BelongsToOrganization` trait + `unread` scope, controller scoped to `user_id` (users only see own notifications), `firstOrFail` prevents ID enumeration.
• *Comment notifications* — fires to assignee AND requester (excluding the commenter themselves). Smart dedup check (`requester_id !== assignee_id`).
• *Frontend SLA badge* — clean color mapping, handles null/no-policy gracefully, shows percent remaining for warnings.
• *NotificationBell* — 30s polling, click-outside to close, optimistic mark-as-read, unread count badge. Well-built.
• *ActivityTimeline* — clean vertical feed with dots and action icons.
• *Claim button* — gated by `isAgent()`, only shows on unassigned tickets.

_Issues found:_

:large_yellow_circle: *P1 — SLA calc runs a DB query per ticket in list views*
`getSlaStatusAttribute()` does `SlaPolicy::where(...)->first()` on every ticket access. On the list page with 15 tickets, that's 15 extra queries. Consider eager-loading the `slaPolicy` relationship or caching the policy lookup.

:large_yellow_circle: *P2 — No notification when ticket is claimed*
Claiming creates an activity log but doesn't notify anyone. The requester might want to know their ticket was picked up. Minor design choice.

:large_yellow_circle: *P3 — `markAsRead` uses route-model-binding bypass*
`POST /notifications/{id}/read` uses `$id` directly instead of route-model binding. Works correctly but inconsistent with the rest of the API which uses `{ticket}` implicit binding. Also, the `{id}` parameter could collide with route ordering if not careful.

:large_green_circle: *P4 — No index on `notifications(user_id, read_at)`*
For production scale, a composite index would speed up the unread count query. Fine for current scope.

:large_green_circle: *P5 — NotificationBell polls even when tab is inactive*
30s interval runs regardless of tab focus. Minor battery/API waste. Could use `document.hidden` check. Low priority.

:large_green_circle: *P6 — SLA doesn't account for business hours*
SLA calc uses wall-clock time (24/7). Real helpdesks often exclude weekends/off-hours. Acceptable for current scope — noted for future enhancement.

_Multi-tenancy check:_ :white_check_mark: Notifications use `BelongsToOrganization` trait + global scope. Claim/assign endpoints go through `authorize('update')` which checks org match. Notification queries are scoped to `user_id` so cross-tenant leakage is impossible.

_Recommendation:_ P1 (N+1 query on SLA) is the most impactful for performance. Others are polish/future scope. Ship it.
